case_metadata.court: SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK, Appellate Division, Fourth Judicial Department

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiff commenced this medical malpractice action after she sustained a perforated bowel during a right salpingo-oophorectomy performed by defendant Julie A. Madejski, M.D., an obstetrician/gynecologist (OB/GYN), on August 27, 2013.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff sustained a perforated bowel during surgery on August 27, 2013.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The surgery was done laparoscopically using robotic assistance, and the perforation went undetected for several days.
evidence_index.summary: Surgery was laparoscopic with robotic assistance; perforation undetected for days.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: A few hours after she was discharged from defendant Eastern Niagara Hospital, Inc. (ENH), plaintiff contacted Dr. Madejski and reported severe abdominal pain and fever, and Dr. Madejski instructed her to go to ENH's emergency room.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff reported pain and fever post-discharge; instructed to return to ER.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Emergency room physician defendant Thomas Michael Kowalak, M.D., wrote an order for a CT scan with oral and rectal contrast, but ENH's radiologic technologist who performed the CT scan on August 28, 2013, did so with only oral and IV contrast.
evidence_index.summary: CT scan ordered with oral and rectal contrast; performed with oral and IV contrast.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The expert opined that performing the surgery in a laparoscopic fashion was contraindicated for plaintiff and that performing the surgery in an open fashion was the required method given plaintiff's risk factors.
evidence_index.summary: Expert opined laparoscopic surgery was contraindicated; open surgery was required.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We agree with plaintiff that the court erred in sua sponte dismissing the complaint and cross-claims against Dr. Kowalak, and we therefore modify the order by vacating the third ordering paragraph and reinstating the complaint and any cross-claims against him.
evidence_index.summary: Court erred in dismissing claims against Dr. Kowalak; claims reinstated.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiff alleged that Dr. Madejski, Artemis, Dr. Loftus, and ENH failed to ensure that rectal contrast was given to plaintiff for the CT scan.
evidence_index.summary: Allegation of failure to ensure rectal contrast for CT scan.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiff's expert general surgeon opined that, in light of Dr. Madejski's concerns of a potential perforation, it was a deviation from the standard of care not to undertake any reasonable efforts to ensure that her directive to have rectal contrast administered was followed.
evidence_index.summary: Expert opined failure to ensure rectal contrast was a deviation from standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We further agree with plaintiff on her cross-appeal that the court erred in granting that part of the motion of Dr. Madejski and Artemis for summary judgment dismissing the complaint against them to the extent that the complaint, as amplified by the bill of particulars, alleged that Dr. Madejski was negligent in her postoperative care of plaintiff.
evidence_index.summary: Court erred in dismissing claims of negligence in postoperative care against Dr. Madejski.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

verdict_detected.key_phrase: We therefore further modify the order accordingly.

initial_state.chief_complaint: Severe abdominal pain and fever post-surgery
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient underwent a right salpingo-oophorectomy with robotic assistance and was discharged. She later reported severe abdominal pain and fever.

requestables.name: CT scan with oral and rectal contrast
requestables.description: CT scan to assess for bowel perforation

reveal.result_summary: Performed with oral and IV contrast only

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ER with severe abdominal pain post-surgery
decision_points.prompt: What imaging should be performed to assess for bowel perforation?

options.description: Order CT scan with oral and rectal contrast
options.clinical_reasoning: Rectal contrast is necessary to detect bowel perforation

options.description: Order CT scan with oral and IV contrast
options.clinical_reasoning: IV contrast alone may not adequately reveal perforation

actual_action_defendant.description: CT scan performed with oral and IV contrast

expected_action_court.description: CT scan should have been performed with rectal contrast
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated rectal contrast was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to ensure rectal contrast was a deviation from standard of care
explanation.what_should_have_happened: CT scan with rectal contrast should have been performed

decision_points.clinical_context: Decision on surgical approach for right salpingo-oophorectomy
decision_points.prompt: What surgical approach should be used given the patient's risk factors?

options.clinical_reasoning: Open surgery was required due to patient's risk factors

options.description: Perform laparoscopic surgery with robotic assistance

actual_action_defendant.description: Laparoscopic surgery with robotic assistance was performed

expected_action_court.description: Open surgery should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated open surgery was required

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Open surgery should have been performed

key_events.event: Patient reports severe abdominal pain and fever

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Room Evaluation
timeline_phases.description: Patient evaluated in ER for postoperative complications

patient_outcome.description: Patient sustained a perforated bowel that went undetected for several days

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to ensure rectal contrast for CT scan
Breach of duty in choosing laparoscopic approach

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to ensure rectal contrast and inappropriate surgical approach
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Rectal contrast would have detected perforation; open surgery was required

factual_timeline.event: CT scan performed with oral and IV contrast

tests_performed.result: Performed with oral and IV contrast

allegations.allegation: Failure to ensure rectal contrast for CT scan
